body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: no
irrlicht_observes: n/a

## Reasoning

The scenario's primitive is `context_curation` / a **compaction command** (e.g. Claude Code `/compact`): a user-invoked, in-place summarization that produces a visible `ready → working → ready` arc. aider has no such command.

- No `/compact` (or equivalent) slash command exists. aider 0.86.2's full command set (`def cmd_*` in `commands.py`) is: add, architect, ask, chat_mode, clear, code, commit, context, copy, copy_context, diff, drop, edit, editor, editor_model, exit, git, help, lint, load, ls, map, map_refresh, model, models, multiline_mode, paste, quit, read_only, reasoning_effort, report, reset, run, save, settings, test, think_tokens, tokens, undo, voice, weak_model, web. The closest are `/clear` and `/reset` (discard history outright) and `/tokens` / `/drop` (report or shrink context) — none summarize the conversation in place via an LLM call, which is what the scenario asserts. The official in-chat-commands docs confirm "No `/compact` command exists."
- aider DOES have an analogous capability, but it is **automatic and background-threaded**, not a user-invoked command. `ChatSummary.too_big()` (`history.py`) trips once chat history crosses `--max-chat-history-tokens` (arg help: "Soft limit on tokens for chat history, after which summarization begins"), and `summarize_start()` (`base_coder.py:1002`) spawns a `threading.Thread` that summarizes via the **weak** model off the REPL's main path. The REPL never blocks on it and never enters a distinct foreground state for it. Because the scenario as written requires a deliberate compaction *command* with a foreground `working` arc, the honest verdict against the canonical primitive is `agent_supports: no` — the automatic mechanism is documented in the caveat below rather than upgraded to `yes`.

## irrlicht_observes: n/a

Derived as n/a because the agent lacks the command-form feature; but note that even the automatic summarizer would be unobservable. The aider adapter (`agent.go`) is `FilesUnderCWD{Filename: ".aider.chat.history.md", RawLineParser}` — the parser only sees lines written to that markdown file: `####` user prompts, assistant prose, `> Tokens:` / `> Model:` / `> Applied edit` / `> Running` blockquotes, and LLM-error blockquotes (`parser.go`). The summarizer never calls `io.append_chat_history` (all `append_chat_history` callers in `io.py` are user-prompt / assistant-output / tool-output writes); it only swaps the in-memory `done_messages`. The verbose `"Starting/Finished summarizing chat history"` strings go to terminal `tool_output`, not the history file. So no event lands in the watched transcript, and the parser would emit no state arc for compaction — irrlicht's `CompactionState` domain field is dead code regardless.

## What would flip the verdict

If aider shipped a user-invoked `/compact`-style command that wrote a recognizable marker to `.aider.chat.history.md`, `agent_supports` would become `yes` and `irrlicht_observes` could be re-judged against that marker.
caveats: aider DOES auto-summarize chat history once it exceeds --max-chat-history-tokens (ChatSummary in history.py), but it runs in a background thread via the weak model with no foreground working arc and no user command — so it is not the scenario's compaction-command primitive.

The auto-summarizer writes nothing to .aider.chat.history.md, so even if the scenario were re-scoped to automatic compaction it would remain unobservable with today's FilesUnderCWD-on-markdown transport.

sources.note: Full in-chat command list; confirms no /compact command — /clear and /reset discard history, /tokens and /drop manage context but do not summarize
aider 0.86.2 cmd_* set has no cmd_compact; closest are cmd_clear / cmd_reset / cmd_tokens / cmd_drop
ChatSummary.too_big() + summarize() — automatic, weak-model summarization of chat history
summarize_start() at line 1002 spawns a background threading.Thread; the REPL never enters a foreground working state for it
--max-chat-history-tokens help: 'Soft limit on tokens for chat history, after which summarization begins'
Source is FilesUnderCWD{.aider.chat.history.md, RawLineParser} — parser only sees lines in the markdown history file
Recognized line kinds: #### prompts, > Tokens/Model/Applied edit/Running, error blockquotes — no compaction marker; turn arcs come from these + IdleFlush
